title: Scripting for Cybersecurity
catalogDescription: An introductory programming/scripting class for students who have no prior programming experience with a focus on Cybersecurity and automation. Covers flow control, loops, functions, using libraries, version control, and APIs.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: MATH 72 with a grade of 2.5 or higher; or eligible for MATH 97 or higher; or instructor consent.

title: CompTIA Security+ Certification Preparation
catalogDescription: A preparation course for the CompTIA Security+ certification, an IT industry recognized entry level certification. Provides students with a broad foundation of network security concepts. Topics include security fundamentals, overview of cryptography, security policies and procedures, common types of attacks, and how to implement network security measures. Covers all current CompTIA Security+ certification exam objectives.

catalogDescription: Covers the management of database tables, queries, views, indexes, and constraints using a Database Management System (DBMS) and Structured Query Language (SQL). Hands-on exercises include the analysis, troubleshooting, and problem solving of real world database scenarios.

title: Routing and Switching Essentials
catalogDescription: Focuses on the knowledge and skills to implement and configure routing and switching. Topics include device configuration, IPv4, IPv6, Subnetting, virtual local area networks (VLANs), routing protocols, and wide area networking (WAN) technologies.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: IT 131 or instructor consent.

catalogDescription: Covers a variety of cybersecurity topics including: security principles, network security and endpoint security concepts, vulnerability assessment and risk management, and incident handling. prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: IT 131 or instructor consent.

title: Routing and Switching in the Enterprise
catalogDescription: Builds upon the knowledge and skills attained in IT 210 . Covers advanced routing using Open Shortest Path First (OSPF), Router Fail-over, inter-VLAN routing, link aggregation, Software Defined Networking, and VPNs. Hands-on exercises include configuration, installation, and troubleshooting.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: IT 210 ; and admission into the IT Bachelor's in Cybersecurity and Networking program; or instructor consent.
